regions.text: Automatic doors in hotels use 'electric eyes.' When a person blocks the light, the door opens.
In a bank, an alarm bell rings if someone touches a window. This keeps safes and money protected from thieves.
Automatic watchers keep offices and stores safe at all times.
ringing: sound of a bell. safe: secure box for money. hotel: place to stay. FUTURE: will ring, touch, break. PRESENT: ring(s), touch(es), break(s). PAST: rang/rung, touched, broke/broken.
